details: The "Lalima Abhiyaan" scheme of the Women and Child Development Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh, aims to improve the health of children, adolescent girls, and women of reproductive age. The scheme focuses on preventing anaemia and raising nutrition awareness among adolescent girls, pregnant women, and lactating mothers. It has been launched to address the widespread issue of anaemia linked to poor nutrition and lack of proper food.
benefits: Free iron folic acid tablets are provided to combat anemia among children, adolescent girls, and women.
Awareness campaigns promote healthy diets and better nutrition practices, especially for adolescent girls and mothers.
The campaign is supported by multiple departments, including Public Health, AYUSH, and School Education, ensuring wide outreach.
The campaign works in a focused, large-scale effort to quickly address anemia and improve health outcomes.
eligibility: The applicant should be one of the following -
Child in the age group of 6 months to 10 years.
Woman of reproductive age group (19 to 49 years).

offline: The interested applicant should visit their nearest Anganwadi center or school, and request a hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the staff exclusively entrusted to issue and collect filled-in applications.

In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents, within the prescribed period (if any), to the Anganwadi worker or school authority.

Request a receipt or acknowledgement from the concerned authority to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).
Ensure that the application is submitted within the prescribed period, if any.

documents_required: Proof of Pregnancy or Lactation (For Pregnant or Lactating Women)

faqs.question: What is the Lalima Abhiyaan?
faqs.answer: The Lalima Abhiyaan is a health initiative aimed at preventing anemia and improving nutrition among children, adolescent girls, pregnant women, and women of reproductive age in Madhya Pradesh.

faqs.question: How can I apply for the Lalima Abhiyaan?
faqs.answer: You can apply by visiting your nearest Anganwadi center or school to fill out an application form.

faqs.question: Is there a registration fee for this scheme?
faqs.answer: No, there is no fee for registration. The Lalima Abhiyaan is completely free of cost.

faqs.question: Will iron folic acid tablets be provided for free?
faqs.answer: Yes, free iron folic acid tablets will be distributed to eligible beneficiaries as part of the Lalima Abhiyaan.

faqs.question: Where can I get the iron folic acid tablets?
faqs.answer: The tablets will be distributed through Anganwadi centers, schools, and healthcare institutions such as local hospitals.

faqs.question: How will I know if I am eligible for the scheme?
faqs.answer: Eligibility will be determined based on your age, health condition (pregnant/lactating), and school enrollment. Verification will be done by the local authorities.

faqs.question: What is the main goal of the Lalima Abhiyaan?
faqs.answer: The main goal is to prevent anemia, improve nutritional awareness, and ensure better health for women, children, and adolescent girls.

faqs.question: How will this scheme help prevent anemia?
faqs.answer: The scheme provides iron folic acid tablets, promotes better nutrition, and educates beneficiaries about a healthy diet to prevent and treat anemia.
